title: Data Analytics Fundamentals
description: A comprehensive introduction to the field of data analytics. Learn the technical lifecycle of data, from extraction and cleaning to analysis and visualization, using modern methodologies.

seo_metadata.title: Data Analytics Fundamentals Certificate | ProCerix
seo_metadata.description: Start your career in Data Analytics. Learn data extraction, cleaning methodologies, exploratory data analysis, and visualization principles.

learning_outcomes: Understand the end-to-end data analytics lifecycle
Identify common data quality issues and cleaning methodologies
Perform Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) to uncover trends
Apply basic statistical measures to summarize datasets
Choose appropriate visualization types for different data sets

modules.title: The Data Landscape
modules.description: Understanding data types and the analytics workflow.

lessons.title: The Data Lifecycle
lessons.content:
From Collection to Insight

The analytics process involves Collection, Preparation, Exploration, Modeling, and Communication. Each step is critical; skipping preparation usually leads to flawed models.

lessons.title: Data Types and Structures
lessons.content:
Structured vs. Unstructured Data

Structured data (like SQL databases) is highly organized. Unstructured data (like text or video) requires different tools. We primarily focus on structured, tabular data in basic analytics.

lessons.title: Data Cleaning Principles
lessons.content:
Garbage In, Garbage Out

Data cleaning involves handling missing values (imputation or deletion), removing duplicates, and standardizing formats (e.g., ensuring all dates follow YYYY-MM-DD).

lessons.title: Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)
lessons.content:
Getting to Know Your Data

EDA is an approach to analyzing data sets to summarize their main characteristics, often using visual methods and basic statistics (mean, median, mode, standard deviation).

lessons.title: Choosing the Right Chart
lessons.content:
Visual Best Practices

Use Line charts for trends over time, Bar charts for comparisons across categories, and Scatter plots to show relationships between two variables. Avoid 3D pie charts.

lessons.title: Building Dashboards
lessons.content:
Effective Reporting

A good dashboard provides an at-a-glance view of KPIs. It should be uncluttered, updated regularly, and tailored to the specific audience (e.g., executives vs. operations teams).

mcqs.question: What does the phrase 'Garbage In, Garbage Out' refer to in data analytics?

options.content: You must delete old data regularly
Flawed or dirty input data will produce flawed analysis results

mcqs.question: Which of the following is an example of Unstructured Data?

options.content: An Excel spreadsheet of sales figures
A collection of customer review text emails

mcqs.question: What is Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) primarily used for?

options.content: To present final reports to the CEO
To summarize the main characteristics of a dataset and find initial patterns

mcqs.question: Which statistical measure represents the middle value of a sorted dataset?
Which chart type is best for displaying trends over a continuous period of time?

options.content: Replacing missing data with substituted values (like the mean)

mcqs.question: Why are 3D Pie Charts generally discouraged in data visualization?

options.content: They are too hard to program
They distort the data and make it difficult to compare slice sizes accurately
They use too much ink when printed
They can only show two variables

mcqs.question: Which visualization is best for showing the relationship or correlation between two numerical variables?
In the data lifecycle, what step must occur before Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)?
What is the primary purpose of a Data Dashboard?

options.content: To store backup copies of raw data
To provide an at-a-glance visual overview of Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)
To perform complex statistical hypothesis testing
